Load Rate : 5,000 mt pwwd of 24 consecutive hours shinc with exceptions on laytime to apply as per cp
Disch Rate : 5,000 mt per crane/pwwdwp of 24 consecutive hours, shinc, basis minimum 4 cranes & minimum 5 holds working simultaneously, prorate if less exceptions on laytime to apply as per cp

Otherwise as per charterers’ terms/conditions
(non-negotiable)...
Note :-
-- Vessel max loa 170 meters
-- G A should always provide:
-- Height of the cranes / air draft from top of the crane
-- Distance of crane from mid structure to railings
-- Latest aerial photos of vessel upper deck from port side and starboard side
-- Updated Q88 / Baltic Q
Refer check list highlighted below for loading loggers at Ruwais which is to be confirmed and complied by the vessel/master on arrival load port Ruwais.
The maximum WL to Main deck should not exceed 6.80 meters, after ballasting No.3 Hold and filling all other ballast tanks. Therefore, it is crucial for the vessel to arrive at the CBDC terminal with full ballast and No.3 Cargo Hold (ballast hold) full, The distance from the waterline to the timber stanchions (fixed) should be Less than at No.1 hold at 15.45 meters, No.3 hold at 15.19 meters, and No.5 hold at 15.10 meters. The vessel’s arrival drafts should be around f/6.30 meters and A/6.80 meters, with a stern trim of 0.50 meters.
Cargo of 15000MT can be distributed between No 2/4 and 5 hold and pump out ballast after departure from Ruwais (summer drafts permitting
++
Pleased to hear.
Thanks & Best Regards
